course.title: Learn Python
course.description: Master the world's most versatile programming language.

chapter.title: Control Flow
chapter.description: Learn how to make decisions in your code using if statements and loops.

lessons.title: If, Elif, and Else Statements
objectives: Learn how to use if statements to control program flow
Understand boolean expressions and comparison operators
Learn to chain conditions with elif and else
lessons.explanation:
Often, you want your code to execute only if a certain condition is true. This is where `if` statements come in.

Python relies on indentation (whitespace at the beginning of a line) to define scope in the code. Other programming languages often use curly-brackets for this purpose.

An `if` statement evaluates a condition. If the condition is True, the indented block of code runs. You can also use `elif` (else if) for additional conditions, and `else` for a catch-all when no conditions are met.
lessons.analogy: Control flow is like a fork in a road. If the weather is sunny, take the path to the beach. Else if it's raining, take the path to the museum. Else, stay home.
common_mistakes: Forgetting the colon (`:`) at the end of the if statement line.
Incorrect indentation. All code inside the if block must be indented by exactly 4 spaces (or 1 tab).
best_practices: Keep your conditional logic as simple as possible. Deeply nested if statements are hard to read.
lessons.summary: You can use `if`, `elif`, and `else` statements alongside comparison operators (like `==`, `>`, `<`) to make decisions in your code.

exercises.statement: Write an if statement that checks if `score` is greater than or equal to 50. If it is, print 'Pass'. Otherwise, print 'Fail'.
exercises.starter_code:
score = 75

# Write your if statement below
